title: fagraea larutensis
intro: Fagraea larutensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, commonly known as the fragrant frangipani. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which are rich in alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. Fagraea larutensis has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as pain relief and immune system support. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness their medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
